quotes.text: Wyoming has fewer people than most American cities, and I want you to know we're aware of this. We've discussed it. We've decided we're fine.
quotes.speaker: Liz Cheney
quotes.context: Speech at the University of Wyoming College of Law, Laramie, October 2017
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Cheney has a dry, matter-of-fact wit and often leans into Wyoming's smallness with genuine pride rather than apology, which makes this land perfectly in her register. The giveaway is the rhythmic three-sentence structure, which is a little too composed for a spontaneous aside.

quotes.text: I did read the opposition research on myself before I ran. My wife said that was either very brave or very stupid, and she hasn't fully decided which.
quotes.speaker: Tim Kaine
quotes.context: Appearance on Pod Save America, recorded in Washington D.C., September 2018
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Kaine's self-effacing, folksy humor and his habit of name-checking his wife Anne make this feel authentic to his public persona. The subtle tell is that real opposition research is rarely something a politician jokes about this directly, even affectionately.

quotes.text: I've been in the Senate long enough to remember when a filibuster meant you actually had to stand there and talk. Some of my colleagues seem to miss those days. I am not one of them.
quotes.speaker: Patrick Leahy
quotes.context: Senate Judiciary Committee hearing, C-SPAN, February 2013
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Leahy's seniority and his dry Vermont temperament make this kind of procedural joke entirely natural for him. The giveaway is the punchy final sentence, which is a beat too clean for off-the-cuff committee remarks.

quotes.text: I have now come to the conclusion never again to think of marrying, and for this reason; I can never be satisfied with anyone who would be blockhead enough to have me.
quotes.speaker: Abraham Lincoln
quotes.context: A candidly self-mocking line from a young Lincoln's letter, poking fun at his own eligibility as a suitor.

quotes.text: I've been called a lot of things over the years. Most of them were on yard signs, which I suppose is a form of fame.
quotes.speaker: Bob Dole
quotes.context: Remarks at the American Enterprise Institute annual dinner, Washington D.C., March 2000
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Dole's famously dry, self-deprecating wit and his comfort with being a political punching bag after 1996 make this sound completely natural. The giveaway is the phrase 'a form of fame,' which is a half-beat more wistful than Dole's typical quick delivery.

quotes.text: It is clear that we do not have the independent legal authority to enforce this warrant.
quotes.speaker: Zohran Mamdani
quotes.context: NYC Mayor Mamdani backing off his promise to arrest Netanyahu, Tuesday statement

source.title: Politics — Mamdani says he doesn’t have legal authority to arrest Netanyahu
